title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial loss.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strict access controls, multi-factor authentication, and regular security audits.
Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to identify and mitigate ransomware activity in cloud environments.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored offline to facilitate recovery in the event of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Trendnet TEW-635BRM up to 1.00.03. This vulnerability...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Trendnet TEW-635BRM up to 1.00.03. This vulnerability affects the function ipoa_test of the file /sbin/rc of the component IPoA WAN Connection Setup. Performing a manipulation of the argument ipoa_ipaddr results in command injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor explains: "We are unable to confirm if the vulnerability exists. This item has been EOL sinc

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands on affected Trendnet TEW-635BRM devices, potentially compromising the device and any connected networks. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or further attacks on internal systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately disconnect affected Trendnet TEW-635BRM devices from the network and replace them with supported and secure alternatives, as the device is End-of-Life (EOL) and will not receive patches.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices and reduce the risk of lateral movement in case of compromise.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts targeting this vulnerability.

The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure.

title: A vulnerability was found in Eleveo Call Recording Software 9.7.0. This affects an unknown part...
description: A vulnerability was found in Eleveo Call Recording Software 9.7.0. This affects an unknown part of the file /callrec/pci_dss_status.jsp. Performing a manipulation results in improper authorization. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Eleveo Call Recording Software 9.7.0 allows for improper authorization, which could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive call recording data. Since the exploit is publicly available, the risk of exploitation increases, although the severity is rated as low.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply access control measures such as restricting access to the affected endpoint (/callrec/pci_dss_status.jsp) via firewall rules or network segmentation.
Monitor for unusual activity or unauthorized access attempts targeting the vulnerable endpoint.
Contact the vendor for updates or patches and consider upgrading to a newer version if available.

title: A weakness has been identified in MiniTool Partition Wizard up to 13.6. The affected element is...
description: A weakness has been identified in MiniTool Partition Wizard up to 13.6. The affected element is an unknown function in the library pwdrvio.sys of the component Signed Kernel Driver. This manipulation causes improper access controls. The attack can only be executed locally. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the signed kernel driver (pwdrvio.sys) of MiniTool Partition Wizard up to version 13.6 allows improper access controls, potentially enabling local attackers to escalate privileges or manipulate system resources. However, the attack requires local access, which limits its scope.
recommended_actions: Upgrade MiniTool Partition Wizard to version 13.9 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict physical and local access to systems running vulnerable versions of the software.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and ensure endpoint protection solutions are up-to-date.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a memory leak vulnerability in the VIFF encoder when memory...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a memory leak vulnerability in the VIFF encoder when memory allocation fails. Attackers can trigger allocation failures by processing specially crafted VIFF images to exhaust available memory and cause denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ImageMagick's VIFF encoder can be exploited to cause a denial of service by exhausting system memory. This could lead to service disruptions, particularly in systems processing untrusted or user-supplied image files.
recommended_actions: Update ImageMagick to version 7.1.2-26 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement resource limits on memory usage for processes handling image files to mitigate potential denial-of-service attacks.
Monitor and restrict the processing of untrusted or user-supplied VIFF image files until the patch is applied.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a use-after-free vulnerability in the FormatMagickCaption...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a use-after-free vulnerability in the FormatMagickCaption method when memory allocation fails. Attackers can trigger memory allocation failures to cause a dangling pointer to reference freed memory, potentially enabling denial of service or code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The use-after-free vulnerability in ImageMagick could allow attackers to exploit memory allocation failures, potentially leading to denial of service (DoS) or remote code execution. This poses a significant risk to systems processing untrusted image files.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to ImageMagick version 7.1.2-26 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization to reduce the risk of processing malicious image files.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or crashes that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-19 contains a heap buffer overflow vulnerability in the magnify...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-19 contains a heap buffer overflow vulnerability in the magnify operation that allows attackers to read out of bounds memory. An unrecognized magnify:method value triggers an out of bounds read, potentially exposing sensitive information or causing denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56372.

title: Parse Server is affected by a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in versions >= 9.0...
description: Parse Server is affected by a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in versions >= 9.0.0, < 9.10.0-alpha.2 and <= 8.6.83. When an uploaded file's extension is not recognized by the mime package, Parse Server preserves the client-supplied Content-Type. A malformed Content-Type that is not a valid type/subtype media type (e.g., 'image', 'image/', or 'image//svg+xml') bypasses the fileUpload.fileExtensions blocklist and is stored unchanged. On storage adapters that persist and serve the u

title: PraisonAI before 1.6.78 contains a remote code execution vulnerability in CodeAgent...
description: PraisonAI before 1.6.78 contains a remote code execution vulnerability in CodeAgent._execute_python() that executes LLM-generated Python code without AST validation, import restrictions, or sandbox enforcement. Attackers can influence LLM output through prompt injection to exfiltrate all environment secrets and execute arbitrary code on the host system.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-61447.

title: The Grav Admin2 plugin (getgrav/grav-plugin-admin2) before 2.0.4 embeds a global JavaScript...
description: The Grav Admin2 plugin (getgrav/grav-plugin-admin2) before 2.0.4 embeds a global JavaScript variable window.__GRAV_CONFIG__ in the Admin2 SPA bootstrap page at /grav/admin (and its subroutes). This object is returned in every unauthenticated response and discloses the server URL, API prefix, admin base path, runtime environment type, and exact Grav and Admin2 version numbers, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to fingerprint the deployment and select version-specific exploits without reconnais

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Grav Admin2 plugin exposes sensitive configuration details, including server URL, API prefix, admin base path, runtime environment type, and exact software versions. This information can be leveraged by attackers to fingerprint the system and launch targeted exploits, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, or further compromise of the server.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Grav Admin2 plugin to version 2.0.4 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement server-side security measures, such as Web Application Firewalls (WAF), to monitor and block suspicious requests.
Review and restrict access to the /grav/admin endpoint to authorized users only, and consider adding additional authentication layers.

title: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a heap use-after-free vulnerability caused by missing null...
description: ImageMagick before 7.1.2-26 contains a heap use-after-free vulnerability caused by missing null check when parsing XMP profiles. Attackers can craft malicious image files with specially crafted XMP data to trigger the vulnerability and cause application crashes.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ImageMagick before version 7.1.2-26 allows attackers to exploit a heap use-after-free issue by crafting malicious image files with specially crafted XMP data. This can lead to application crashes and potentially allow further exploitation, such as arbitrary code execution, depending on the environment.
recommended_actions: Immediately update ImageMagick to version 7.1.2-26 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization for uploaded image files to prevent malicious payloads from being processed.
Monitor application logs for unusual crashes or behavior that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-14570 - Crypt::DSA versions before 1.22 for Perl draw the DSA signing nonce and private key from a biased ra
description:
Crypt::DSA versions before 1.22 for Perl draw the DSA signing nonce and private key from a biased random generator, leading to private-key recovery.

"Crypt::DSA::Util::makerandom forces the high bit of every value it returns to obtain an exactly N-bit integer for prime search. The signing nonce and the private key are drawn from makerandom. Because the high bit is always set, the result is not uniform: its top bit is fixed, producing insecure values."

An attacker who collects a modest number of signatures under an affected key, together with the public key, can recover the private key with a lattice attack.

Keys used to sign with an affected version should be considered compromised and new keys should be generated.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-14570.
